label: Northern Metropolis legal framework scheduled for Hong Kong Legislative Council in March
label_zh: 北部都會區法律框架預計於三月提交香港立法會

timeline.title: Hong Kong Unveils Ecological Guidelines as Legislative Preparations Intensify for Northern Metropolis Development Framework
timeline.title_zh: 香港公布生態指南，加緊推進北部都會區發展框架立法準備工作
timeline.sub_title: The Hong Kong government released first-of-their-kind nature-based design guidelines today, marking a significant step in the Northern Metropolis strategy before the new flexible legal framework reaches the legislature on March 24.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 香港政府今日發布首創的基於自然的設計指南，標誌著北部都會區戰略邁出重要一步，此前新的靈活法律框架將於 3 月 24 日提交立法。
timeline.summary:
The development of Hong Kong's Northern Metropolis entered a new phase of implementation today with the official release of the city's first Nature-based Solutions Design Guidelines (HKNbSDG). Jointly developed by the Civil Engineering and Development Department and the Agriculture, Fisheries and Conservation Department, the guidelines are positioned as a central resource for advancing the Northern Metropolis development strategy. [ref:1, 2, 7] By integrating ecological science into urban design, the framework aims to foster the co-existence of high-density development and environmental conservation across the 30,000-hectare project area. This launch follows a series of high-level government announcements indicating that the legal and financial structures for the megaproject are reaching a decisive stage this month.

Secretary for Development Bernadette Linn Hon-ho confirmed that a relatively flexible legal framework for the Northern Metropolis is scheduled to be introduced to the Legislative Council’s Panel on Development on March 24, 2026. This dedicated legislation is designed to fast-track the megaproject by removing traditional planning barriers and streamlining statutory procedures. The [ref:6, 4] proposed law will focus on empowering the government to exercise greater flexibility in land-use approvals and the introduction of subsidiary legislation. Such [ref:6] measures are intended to allow for faster adjustments to plot ratios and specific land uses, catering to the needs of major enterprises and technology firms looking to establish a presence in the region.

The legal framework is expected to target six primary areas: the establishment of statutory industry park companies, the creation of dedicated funding channels, management of cross-boundary flows, acceleration of building plan approvals, relaxation of zoning parameters, and the expediting of land resumption compensation. By allowing some funding approvals to bypass the traditional line-item legislative process, the government aims to ensure that project-specific investment decisions can be made with the agility required for a global technology hub. Officials have emphasized that while the system will offer more discretion, it will remain a structured and rule-based environment to maintain transparency.

Financially, the project has received a significant boost from the latest city budget, which seeks Legislative Council approval for HK$30 billion in initial capital. This funding is earmarked for three cornerstone developments: the Hetao Hong Kong Park, the San Tin Technopole, and the Hung Shui Kiu Industry Park. Authorities [ref:6] are positioning these entities as well-backed operators capable of attracting global consortiums. The government [ref:6] plans to maintain a heavy presence on the boards of these companies to ensure strategic coordination while avoiding a monolithic development model.

The Northern Metropolis is envisioned as a new engine for Hong Kong’s economic growth, eventually housing 2.5 million residents and providing 650,000 jobs. It serves as a critical link to the Greater Bay Area, integrating Hong Kong’s regulatory strengths with Shenzhen’s technological ecosystem. With public consultations ongoing throughout the first quarter of 2026 and a formal bill submission expected by mid-year, the current legislative momentum underscores the government's commitment to transforming the New Territories into a premier international innovation and technology city.
timeline.summary_zh:
香港北部都會區的發展今日進入新的實施階段，城市首創的基於自然的解決方案設計指南 (HKNbSDG) 正式發布。該指南由土木工程拓展署及漁農自然護理署聯合編製，被定位為推進北部都會區發展戰略的核心資源。通過將生態科學融入城市設計，該框架旨在促進佔地 30,000 公頃的項目區域內，高密度發展與環境保護的共存。此發布緊隨一系列高級政府公告，表明該大型項目的法律和財政結構將於本月進入關鍵階段。[ref:1, 2, 7]

發展局局長林鄭月娥證實，北部都會區的相對靈活的法律框架預計將於 2026 年 3 月 24 日提交立法會發展事務委員會。這項專門立法旨在通過消除傳統規劃障礙和簡化法定程序來加快大型項目的進程。擬議法律將側重於賦予政府更大的土地用途批准靈活性和引入附屬法例。這些措施旨在允許更快地調整地積比和特定土地用途，以滿足希望在该地區建立業務的主要企業和科技公司的需求。[ref:6, 4]

該法律框架預計將針對六個主要領域：設立法定工業園區公司、創建專門的資金渠道、管理跨境流動、加速建築計劃審批、放寬分區參數以及加快土地收回賠償。通過允許部分資金批准繞過傳統的逐項立法程序，政府旨在確保項目特定投資決策能夠以全球科技中心所需的敏捷性做出。官員們強調，雖然該系統將提供更多自由裁量權，但仍將是一個結構化和基於規則的環境，以保持透明度。

財政方面，該項目得到了最新城市預算的重大推動，預算尋求立法會批准 300 億港元的初始資本。這筆資金將用於三個基石開發項目：河套香港公園、落馬洲科技園和洪水橋工業邨。當局將這些實體定位為有力的營運商，能夠吸引全球財團。政府計劃在這些公司的董事會中保持重要影響力，以確保戰略協調，同時避免單一化的發展模式。[ref:6]

北部都會區被設想為香港經濟增長的新引擎，最終將容納 250 萬居民並提供 65 萬個就業機會。它作為連接大灣區的關鍵節點，將香港的監管優勢與深圳的技術生態系統相結合。隨著 2026 年第一季度的公眾諮詢持續進行，以及預計年中正式提交法案，當前的立法勢頭凸顯了政府將新界轉變為一流國際創新科技城市的承諾。

timeline.title: Northern Metropolis designated as national hard deliverable as Hong Kong prepares March legal framework
timeline.title_zh: 北京支持高管領導的北部都會區框架，擬於3月24日提交立法
timeline.sub_title: The project is explicitly listed in China's draft 15th Five-Year Plan, coinciding with upcoming Legislative Council discussions to establish a flexible regulatory framework to accelerate development.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 丁薛祥副總理敦促香港通過改進治理加速大型項目開發，政府準備向立法會提交靈活的法律框架。
timeline.summary:
The Northern Metropolis development strategy reached a critical juncture today as it was formally identified as a "hard deliverable" within the draft outline of China's 15th Five-Year Plan (2026–2030). This national endorsement, unveiled during the ongoing legislative sessions in Beijing, mandates accelerated progress for the border-spanning megaproject. In response, Hong Kong Chief Executive John Lee announced that the city will draft its first-ever local five-year development blueprint to ensure seamless alignment with national industrial and technological goals, placing the Northern Metropolis at the heart of the SAR’s economic pivot toward innovation.

Simultaneously, the Hong Kong government is finalizing the "relatively flexible" legal framework promised to the Legislative Council (LegCo). Secretary for Development Bernadette Linn confirmed that this framework is scheduled for a formal discussion at the LegCo Development Panel meeting on March 24, 2026. The proposed [ref:3] legislation aims to deviate from traditional, exhaustive regulatory models. Instead, it [ref:3] will empower the government with the flexibility to streamline planning procedures, adjust plot ratios, and rezone land use more rapidly to accommodate the shifting needs of high-tech enterprises and industrial partners over the project's multi-decade timeline.

Financially, the government is moving to secure the project's foundation with a proposed HK$30 billion (US$3.8 billion) initial capital injection. This funding is earmarked for three primary zones: the Hetao Hong Kong Park, the San Tin Technopole, and the Hung Shui Kiu Industry Park. Financial Secretary [ref:3] Paul Chan highlighted that these funds are essential for establishing the government-owned companies that will oversee these sites, providing the necessary leverage to attract global consortiums and public-private partnerships. The administration is also exploring a record-setting transfer of HK$150 billion from the Exchange Fund to the Capital Works Reserve Fund to support these long-term infrastructure commitments.

Strategically, the project is shifting toward an "industry-driven" model, with approximately 40 percent of the 800 hectares designated for industrial use expected to be ready within the next five years. This aligns with recent directives from Vice Premier Ding Xuexiang, who urged Hong Kong to leverage the Northern Metropolis to deepen integration with the Greater Bay Area. By establishing [ref:2] a dedicated legal structure this month, authorities hope to remove procedural barriers that have historically slowed large-scale developments, ensuring the Northern Metropolis serves as a primary engine for the city's "South-North dual engine" growth strategy.
timeline.summary_zh:
香港正進入其雄心勃勃的北部都會區項目的關鍵立法階段，政府定於3月24日將專門的法律框架提交立法會發展事務委員會。這次即將召開的會議標誌著政府努力加速實施這個佔地30,000公頃的大型項目，該項目旨在成為一個新的國際創新和技術中心。發展局局長林鄭月娥將擬議的立法描述為「相對靈活」，旨在賦予行政部門更大的權力，以繞過傳統官僚障礙並簡化規劃和土地使用批准。

預計該框架將賦予政府引入附屬立法和行使特定權力以加快開發週期的權力。關鍵條款包括簡化土地收回的法定程序，並允許更快地調整地積比率和土地用途。通過降低監管要求的複雜性，政府旨在為大型企業，特別是科技和先進製造業領域的企業，創造一個更具吸引力的環境。官員們指出，該框架將重點放在消除障礙，而不是詳盡的條款，從而使項目能夠適應其數十年實施期間不斷變化的市場條件。

在「兩會」期間獲得北京高層信號後，該立法推動的政治勢頭有所加強。3月8日，中國副總理丁薛祥強調，「行政主導的管治體系」對於香港align（對齊）國家第十五個五年計劃至關重要。在會見當地代表時，丁薛祥特別指出了北部都會區是首要關切的領域，敦促企業家和商界積極支持和投資該大型項目。他的講話強調了北京對「切實成果」和高效治理的期望，並重申了在即將到來的法案中尋求法律權力的必要性。

該項目的財政支持也在城市最新的財政規劃中得到鞏固。2026-27年度預算計劃撥款 iniziale （初步）300億港元，專門用於河套香港園區、新田科技城和洪水橋工業園的開發。此外，財政司司長陳茂波提議從外匯基金撥款1,500億港元至資本工程儲備基金，以確保大型基礎設施的持續資金。這一財政策略旨在增強私營部門的信心，並促進公私營夥伴關係，這是政府「產業導向」發展模式的核心。

隨著3月24日會議的臨近，焦點將仍然是如何在新法律中平衡速度、透明度和財政審慎的需求。北部都會區最終設想為250萬居民提供住房，並創造約50萬個新工作崗位，將城市的經濟中心向北轉移，以便與深圳和粵港澳大灣區更深入地融合。今年晚些時候通過專門立法的成功，被視為將這些戰略目標轉化為切實基礎設施和經濟增長的關鍵機制。
